title: Cloud lane Official-CLI bridge adapter (Claude Code / Codex CLI as transport)
goal: Implement OfficialCliBridgeRuntime as a ModelRuntime adapter that uses an Official CLI (Claude Code, Codex CLI, gemini-cli, etc.) as a transport for model invocations. Operator provides CLI path + auth; adapter spawns the CLI per request, captures output, returns through ModelRuntime trait. Useful when BYOK key is not desired/available.
expected_diff_shape: New src/backend/handshake_core/src/model_runtime/cloud/official_cli_bridge.rs implementing ModelRuntime; CliBridgeConfig per CLI; tests.
unblock_conditions: MT-070 LlmClient routing + MT-125 cloud lane pattern merged.
implementation_notes: OfficialCliBridgeRuntime is a fundamentally different cloud lane: invocations transport through a CLI subprocess rather than a direct HTTP call (operator uses their existing CLI auth, no API key in Handshake). CliBridgeConfig per CLI: `{ cli_kind: CliKind (enum ClaudeCode|CodexCli|GeminiCli|Other), executable_path: PathBuf, args_template: Vec<String> (with {prompt}, {model} placeholders), output_format: CliOutputFormat (Json|RawText|JsonStream), env_vars: HashMap<String,String>, working_dir: Option<PathBuf>, timeout_seconds: u64 }`. Per-request flow: serialise prompt to CLI args (per output_format spec), spawn under SandboxAdapter (cluster B; the CLI is treated as an untrusted subprocess), capture stdout, parse per output_format, yield GeneratedToken stream (or single token for non-streaming CLIs). Cancellation: kill subprocess on cancel_token fire. ProcessOwnershipLedger row with engine_kind=OfficialCliBridge per MT-069. Capabilities for CLI bridge: NONE of the inference techniques (LoRA/KV/steering/etc.) work through a CLI bridge — all capability flags false. The bridge is a usability-not-feature lane for operator workflow flexibility. Authentication is operator-managed (the CLI handles its own auth, not Handshake). Per spec §3.6.4 ExternalEngineImport precedent: bridge marks model with provider=OfficialCli (similar to ExternalCompat semantics). Tests: mock CLI script (a bash echo or `cmd /c echo` on Windows); spawn, parse, yield; cancellation; ProcessOwnershipLedger row.
risk_if_missed: Official-CLI bridge missing; operator must use direct BYOK even when their preferred lane is the CLI subscription.
validator_focus: Subprocess sandboxed; ProcessOwnershipLedger row; all inference capabilities false (no false advertising); cancellation kills child.

minimum_controls: All inference capability flags false (no false advertising on CLI bridge).
Cancellation kills child subprocess (verified via PID disappearance).
